subject: Agricultural Science
preamble: This syllabus has been designed to portray Agricultural Science as an applied science with emphasis on the acquisition of knowledge and skills associated with the content. A general review of the Junior Secondary School Agricultural Science syllabus is presumed. Candidates will be expected to answer questions on all the topics set out in the column headed syllabus. The notes therein are intended to indicate the scope of the questions which will be set, but they are not to be considered as an exhaustive list of limitations and illustration. Every school offering Agricultural Science must establish a farm where crops are grown, keep at least one species of ruminant and one non-ruminant, and establish a fish pond where feasible.
objectives: Stimulate and sustain students' interest in agriculture.
Enable students acquire functional knowledge and practical skills to prepare them for further studies and occupation in agriculture.

examination_scheme.summary: There will be three papers: Papers 1, 2 and 3 all of which must be taken. Papers 1 and 2 will be a composite paper to be taken at one sitting.

papers.title: Multiple Choice Objectives
papers.details: Will consist of fifty multiple choice questions to be answered within 50 minutes for 50 marks.

papers.title: Essay Questions
papers.details: Will consist of six essay questions with each drawn from at least two themes in the syllabus. Candidates will be required to answer five of the questions within 2 hours 10 minutes for 90 marks.

papers.title: Practical Test / Alternative to Practical
papers.details: Will be a practical paper for school candidates and alternative to practical paper for private candidates. It will consist of four questions, all of which should be answered within 1 hour 30 minutes for 60 marks.

topics.title: Meaning and importance of agriculture
details: Definition and branches of agricultural science.
Importance of agriculture to the individual, community and nation.

topics.title: Problems of agricultural development and possible solutions
details: Problems related to land tenure, basic amenities, finance, transportation, storage and processing facilities, agricultural education and extension, tools and machinery, farm inputs, marketing system, and environmental degradation.
Assessment includes incidence of pests and diseases, vagaries of weather, labour, and government policy.
Possible solutions to identified problems.

topics.title: Meaning and differences between subsistence and commercial agriculture
details: Meaning of subsistence and commercial agriculture.
Differences between subsistence and commercial agriculture based on their characteristics.
Advantages and disadvantages of subsistence and commercial agriculture.
Problems of subsistence and commercial agriculture.

topics.title: Roles of government in agricultural development
details: Agricultural finance: credit and subsidy.
Agricultural education.
Agricultural extension services.
Agricultural policies and programmes (e.g., OFN, ADP, Farm Settlement, ASRP, NACS).

topics.title: Role of non-governmental organizations in agricultural development
details: Meaning of non-governmental organizations (NGOs).
Roles of NGOs in agricultural development (e.g., WARDA, IITA, ILCA, ICRISAT).

topics.title: Agricultural laws and reforms
details: Land tenure systems in West Africa.
Government laws on land use in West Africa.
Advantages and disadvantages of the Land Use Act (Decree) and land reforms in West Africa.

topics.title: Meaning and importance of agricultural ecology
details: Meaning of agricultural ecology and ecosystem.
Components of farm ecosystem e.g., biotic and abiotic.
Interactions of components in terrestrial and aquatic agro-ecosystems (mono/sole cropping, mixed cropping, mixed farming, fish ponds, forestry).

topics.title: Land and its uses
details: Meaning and characteristics of land (free gift of nature, immobile, limited in supply).
Agricultural uses: crop production, wildlife conservation, livestock, aquaculture, forestry, apiculture.
Non-agricultural uses: industry, housing, transport, health centres, religious sites, mining, recreational centres, schools, markets.

topics.title: Factors affecting land availability for agricultural purpose
details: Physical factors: soil type, topography, land degradation, soil pollution.
Economic factors: population pressure, industrial expansion, mining, tourism.
Socio-cultural factors: land tenure system, religious purposes.

topics.title: Agro-allied industries and relationship between agriculture and industry
details: Agro-based industries and raw materials (paper, beverage, textile, soap, leather, canning).
Relationship between agriculture and industries: provision of raw materials, market for industrial goods, food for workers.

topics.title: Environmental factors affecting crop and animal distribution and production
details: Climatic factors: rainfall, temperature, light, wind, relative humidity.
Biotic factors: predators, parasites, soil micro-organisms, pests, pathogens, weeds; interrelationships (competition, parasitism, mutualism).
Edaphic factors: soil pH, soil texture, soil structure, soil type.

topics.title: Rock formation
details: Types of rock: igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic.
Processes of rock formation.

topics.title: Soil formation and profile development
details: Factors of soil formation: parent rock, organisms, climate, topography, time.
Processes of soil formation: physical and chemical weathering.
Soil profile development: identification, description, and importance of soil horizons.

topics.title: Types, composition and properties of soil
details: Types of soil and separation into sand, silt, and clay fractions.
Chemical and biological composition: soil macro and micro nutrients, soil water, macro-organisms, microbes, soil air.
Soil pH: determination, causes, and correction of acidity/alkalinity.
Physical properties: texture, structure, water holding capacity, porosity, capillarity, consistency.

topics.title: Plant nutrients and nutrient cycle
details: Macro-nutrients (N, P, K, Ca, Mg, S) and Micro-nutrients (Zn, Fe, Mo, Co, Bo, Cu): functions and deficiency symptoms.
Factors affecting nutrient availability (pH, leaching, crop removal, burning).
Methods of replenishing nutrients: crop rotation, manuring, fertilizer application, fallowing, liming, cover-cropping.
Nutrient cycles: Nitrogen, Carbon, Water, Phosphorus.
Organic agriculture: meaning and importance.

topics.title: Irrigation
details: Meaning and importance of irrigation systems.
Types: overhead (sprinkler), surface (flooding, furrow, basin, border), underground (perforated pipes, drip).
Advantages, disadvantages, and problems associated with irrigation.

topics.title: Drainage
details: Meaning and importance of drainage.
Types of drainage systems: surface (channel, furrow) and subsurface/underground.
Advantages and disadvantages of drainage systems.

topics.title: Agricultural pollution
details: Meaning and causes: excessive agro-chemicals, oil spillage, livestock waste disposal.
Effects on farmers and agricultural productivity.
Ways of minimizing land and pond pollution.

topics.title: Simple farm tools
details: Meaning, types (cutlass, hoe, spade, shovel), identification, uses, and general maintenance.

topics.title: Farm machinery and implements
details: Farm machinery: tractor, bulldozer, shellers, dryers, incubators, milking machines, combine harvester.
Tractor-coupled implements: ploughs, harrows, ridgers, planters, harvesters, sprayers.
Meaning, functions, and identification of parts.

topics.title: Maintenance practices and precautionary measures
details: Reasons for maintenance and routine servicing practices.
Precautionary measures in the use of farm machinery.

topics.title: Agricultural mechanization
details: Meaning and mechanized operations (ploughing, harrowing, planting, harvesting, milking).
Advantages, disadvantages, and limitations of mechanization.

topics.title: Prospects of agricultural mechanization
details: Ways of improving mechanization: developing affordable machinery and establishing agricultural engineering schools.

topics.title: Farm power
details: Sources of farm power.
Advantages and disadvantages of different farm power sources.

topics.title: Farm surveying
details: Meaning, importance, common survey equipment, uses, and maintenance.

topics.title: Farm planning
details: Meaning, factors to consider, and importance of farm planning.

topics.title: Principles of farmstead planning
details: Meaning of farmstead, site selection, location of structures, and sketching layout.

topics.title: Classification of crops
details: Classification based on uses (cereals, pulses, roots/tubers, vegetables).
Classification based on life cycle (annual, biennial, perennial, ephemeral).
Classification based on morphology (monocotyledonous, dicotyledonous).

topics.title: Husbandry of selected crops
details: Husbandry practices for representative crops: Cereals, Pulses, Roots and tubers, Vegetables, Fruits, Beverages, Spices, Oils, Fibres, Latex, and Sugar cane.
Aspects covered: botanical/common names, climatic/soil requirements, propagation, spacing, fertilizer application, pest/disease control, harvesting, and storage.

topics.title: Pasture and forage crops
details: Meaning, uses, types of pasture, and common grasses/legumes.
Factors affecting productivity, establishment, and management practices of pasture.

topics.title: Crop improvement
details: Aims and methods (introduction, selection, breeding).
Genetic terms (traits, chromosomes, genes) and Mendel's 1st and 2nd laws of inheritance.
Advantages and disadvantages of crop improvement.

topics.title: Forest management
details: Meaning, importance of forestry, forest regulations, and management practices.
Implications of deforestation.

topics.title: Agro-forestry practices in West Africa
details: Meaning and systems: taungya system, alley cropping, ley farming.
Identification of suitable tree species.

topics.title: Meaning and importance of ornamental plants
details: Definition and socio-aesthetic importance.

topics.title: Common types of ornamental plants
details: Types according to uses: bedding plants, hedging plants, lawn grasses.
Common and botanical names of examples.

topics.title: Settings and location for planting ornamental plants
details: Site selection and design arrangements.

topics.title: Methods of cultivating ornamental plants
details: Propagation by seed and vegetative methods.

topics.title: Maintenance of ornamental plants
details: Maintenance practices: watering, mulching, pruning.

topics.title: Diseases of crops
details: Meaning and general effects of diseases.
Causal organisms, symptoms, transmission, prevention, and control for fungal, viral, bacterial, and nematode diseases in major crop groupings.

topics.title: Pests of crops
details: Meaning and classification of insect (biting/chewing, piercing/sucking, boring) and non-insect pests (birds, rodents).
Field and storage pests: life cycle, damage, economic importance, and control.
Side effects of control methods (chemical pollution, biological disruption, burning).

topics.title: Weeds
details: Meaning, types, features, and common/botanical names of weeds.
Effects on crops and economic importance.
Control methods: cultural, biological, chemical, physical, and mechanical.

topics.title: Types and classification of farm animals
details: Types: cattle, sheep, goat, poultry, pig, rabbit, fish.
Classification by habitat (terrestrial/aquatic) and uses (food, protection, pet).

topics.title: Anatomy and physiology of farm animals
details: Parts, internal organs, and organ systems (digestive, circulatory, respiratory).
Differences between monogastric and ruminant digestive systems.

topics.title: Animal reproduction
details: Meaning, hormonal roles, and reproductive system structures.
Reproductive processes: oestrus cycle, heat period, mating, gestation, parturition, lactation, colostrum.
Egg formation process in poultry.

topics.title: Environmental physiology
details: Meaning and effects of climatic changes (temperature, humidity, light) on growth, reproduction, milk, and egg production.

topics.title: Livestock management
details: Systems of management: extensive, intensive, semi-intensive.
Housing, feeding, hygiene, record keeping, and finishing of ruminants and non-ruminants.

topics.title: Animal nutrition
details: Classification of feeds and sources/functions of nutrients.
Types of rations: balanced diet, maintenance, and production rations.
Causes, symptoms, and correction of malnutrition (e.g., ketosis, rickets).

topics.title: Rangeland and pasture management
details: Importance, characteristics, and common grasses/legumes in rangeland.
Factors affecting herbage production and methods of rangeland improvement.

topics.title: Animal improvement
details: Aims and methods: introduction, selection, breeding.
Breeds (local, exotic, cross/hybrid) and performance comparisons.
Artificial insemination: techniques, semen collection, advantages, and disadvantages.

topics.title: Animal health management
details: Causal organisms, symptoms, transmission, prevention, and control of livestock diseases (viral, bacterial, fungal, protozoan).
Endoparasites and ectoparasites: life cycles, economic importance, and control.
General preventive measures: quarantine, immunization, sanitation, breeding for resistance.

topics.title: Aquaculture
details: Types: fish, shrimp, and crab farming.
Siting, establishing, stocking, feeding, harvesting, processing, and preserving fish.
Fishery regulations, tools, and fishing methods.

topics.title: Apiculture or bee keeping
details: Types of bees (indigenous/exotic) and importance of apiculture.
Traditional vs. modern methods and bee keeping equipment.
Precautionary measures in apiary management.

topics.title: Basic economic principles
details: Scarcity, choice, scale of preference, and the law of diminishing returns.

topics.title: Factors of production
details: Land, capital, labour, and management/entrepreneur.
Rural-urban migration effects on agricultural labour.

topics.title: Principles of demand
details: Definition, Law of demand, determinants, movements along, and shifts in demand curve.

topics.title: Principles of supply
details: Definition, Law of supply, determinants, movements along, and shifts in supply curve.

topics.title: Implications of demand and supply for agricultural production
details: Price support, price control, and subsidy programmes.

topics.title: Functions of a farm manager
details: Definition, roles, responsibilities, and operational problems faced by farm managers.

topics.title: Agricultural finance
details: Importance, sources of farm finance, and classification of credit (short/medium/long term, institutional/non-institutional, cash/kind).
Problems faced by farmers procuring loans and financial institutions granting credit.
Capital market institutions, stock exchange, and agri-business financing.

topics.title: Farm records and accounts
details: Types: inventory, production, income/expenditure, special records.
Farm accounting: profit & loss, balance sheet, salvage value, appreciation, depreciation, budget.

topics.title: Marketing of agricultural produce
details: Marketing channels, agents, and marketing functions (assembling, transport, processing).
Export crops, export guidelines, corporate bodies (e.g., ANCE), and marketing problems.

topics.title: Agricultural insurance
details: Importance, insurance premiums, and policy types (crop, livestock, vehicle, fire, life).
Problems and limitations in agricultural insurance.

topics.title: Agricultural extension
details: Meaning, extension methods (individual, group, mass contact), and qualities of good extension workers.
Programs in West Africa (ADP, NDE, Agro-service centres) and implementation problems.

topics.title: Agricultural Ecology Practical
details: Manual soil texture evaluation, fertile vs. infertile soil examination, soil profile description.
Rock identification (igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic).
Laboratory work: mechanical sedimentation analysis, bulk density, pore space, moisture content, water holding capacity, wilting point, capillarity, pH determination.
Chemical fertilizers and organic manure preparation (compost, green manure, farmyard).

topics.title: Agricultural Engineering and Mechanization Practical
details: Identification, uses, and maintenance of garden/horticultural tools, farm tractors, and tractor-drawn/animal-drawn implements.
Identification of harvesting, processing, storage, livestock, fishing, and surveying equipment.

topics.title: Crop Production Practical
details: Identification of seeds, seedlings, storage organs, pasture grasses, and weeds.
Identification of field/storage pests, disease causal agents, damage symptoms, and control measures.
Seedbed preparation, germination tests, fertilizer application, mulching, and propagation methods (layering, grafting, budding).

topics.title: Animal Production Practical
details: Breed identification, restraints, grooming, external features, and internal organs (digestive, reproductive, excretory).
Animal by-products, feedstuffs/rations, endoparasites, ectoparasites, and disease prevention methods.
Routine livestock management operations (debeaking, dehorning, castration, ear-notching, tattooing) and fish harvesting/preservation.
